why_it_matters: This ₹450 crore mainboard issue has strong grey-market momentum, although its sharply higher FY2026 debt is a key risk. ([sebi.gov.in](https://www.sebi.gov.in/filings/public-issues/jul-2026/caliber-mining-and-logistics-limited-rhp_102790.html?utm_source=openai))

India’s largest asset manager drew 7.5-times subscription for its roughly ₹9,813 crore OFS, making it the period’s most consequential mainboard IPO. ([moneycontrol.com](https://www.moneycontrol.com/ipo/sbi-funds-management-sfml01-ipodetail?utm_source=openai))

Exceptional grey-market demand reflects enthusiasm for its defence, aerospace and semiconductor exposure, but the BSE SME issue carries elevated liquidity and volatility risk. ([moneycontrol.com](https://www.moneycontrol.com/ipo/millworks-technologies-mtl11-ipodetail?utm_source=openai))

The ₹126.25 crore mainboard fresh issue will fund textile-capacity expansion and debt reduction, but its near-flat GMP indicates muted listing expectations. ([indiainfoline.com](https://www.indiainfoline.com/news/ipo/alpine-texworld-ipo-opens-today-price-band-gmp-dates-financials-and-should-you-apply?utm_source=openai))

The ₹89.76 crore BSE SME issue offers exposure to automated parking infrastructure, though early bidding and the declining GMP suggest cautious demand. ([m.economictimes.com](https://m.economictimes.com/markets/ipos/fpos/sotefin-bharat-ipo-opens-issue-16-subscribed-on-day-one-so-far-gmp-suggests-12-listing-pop/amp_articleshow/132435645.cms?utm_source=openai))

The ₹742 crore power-infrastructure IPO debuted strongly at ₹250 on NSE and ₹269 on BSE—premiums of 16.8% and 25.7%, respectively—after heavy subscription. ([business-standard.com](https://www.business-standard.com/markets/capital-market-news/laser-power-infra-gains-on-market-debut-126071600325_1.html?utm_source=openai))
